data.title: HP plans to acquire Humane, but not its AI pin: report
data.description:
HP Inc. (NYSE:HPQ) plans to acquire the Silicon Valley startup Humane, but the deal does not include its wearable artificial intelligence-powered clip on computer, known as the Ai Pin, according to Bloomberg.

HP is acquiring Humane's software platform and intellectual property for $116M, according to the report. HP also plans to retain most of its employees. However, Humane's Ai pin business is coming to an end. The Humane team is expected to form a new division at HP with the goal of implementing AI tech in HP's products.

The Ai pin was released in November 2023. The device, meant to be worn on clothing, could be tapped to talk to a virtual assistant and beam text and images on a user's palm through a laser projection system. However, the wearable computer, which cost $699 and a $24 per month subscription, racked up numerous bad reviews and was poorly received.

However, the technology behind the pin was considered ahead of its time, and could be implemented into other products, which is what HP reportedly plans to do.

data.title: EVS Broadcast Equipment SA reports FY results
data.description:
• EVS Broadcast Equipment SA press release (OTCPK:EVSBY): FY net profit of  €42.9 million.

  • Revenue of €198 million (+14.3% Y/Y). 

  • The order book reserved for 2025 is estimated at €107 million, growing 6.6% compared to beginning of the year 2024.

data.title: TAV Havalimanlari Holding A.S. reports FY results
data.description:
• TAV Havalimanlari Holding A.S. press release (OTCPK:TAVHY): FY net income of €183 million.
  • Revenue of €1.66 billion and EBITDA of €489 million.
  • The company said it closed the year with a total of 107 million passengers, reflecting an 11% year-over-year growth, and served 71 million international passengers, marking a 13% increase over 2023.
  • For 2025, the company anticipates EBITDA between €520–590 million and also expect to continue deleveraging, targeting a net debt/EBITDA ratio of 2.5–3.0x.

data.title: Colgate-Palmolive to acquire Prime100 owner
data.description:
• Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) announced that it has agreed to acquire Care TopCo Pty Ltd, owner of the Prime100 pet food brand.
  • The transaction will be financed with a combination of debt and cash and is currently expected to close in the second quarter of 2025.
  • The acquisition is conditioned upon receipt of regulatory approval in Australia.
  • The transaction is not expected to have a material impact on diluted earnings per share in 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BrightSpire Q4 earnings drift down, as expected, as loan portfolio activity falls
data.description:
BrightSpire Capital (NYSE:BRSP) on Tuesday delivered Q4 earnings that suffered from a slowdown in portfolio loan activity.

Q4 adjusted EPS of $0.18, matching the consensus, drifted down from $0.21 in the prior quarter.

“The Company had a productive quarter and year highlighted by new loan originations and watchlist resolutions, all while continuing to maintain liquidity," said CEO Michael J. Mazzei. "Looking ahead, we expect to continue to resolve watchlist and REO assets and grow the portfolio and earnings through new loan originations.”

GAAP net book value retreated to $8.08 per share in Q4 from $8.39 in Q3, and undepreciated book value of $8.89 slid from $9.11 over the same period.

At Dec. 31, 20924, the commercial real estate credit REIT's total loans portfolio stood at $2.52B with a total loan count of 76, down from $2.59B at Sept. 30, 2024.

Current expected credit loss reserve of $165.9M at the end of Q4 was up from $76.0M at the end of Q4 2023.

Conference call on Feb. 19 at 10:00 a.m. ET.

data.title: Faraday Future ramps up AI R&D efforts
data.description:
• Faraday Future Intelligent Electric (NASDAQ:FFIE) has begun hiring for key positions to help support its AI initiatives as it furthers its plans to significantly expand AI R&D efforts and input, the company said on Tuesday.
  • Faraday Future also plans to change its Nasdaq ticker symbol to “FFAI” on March 10, 2025, and host an "FF Open AI Day" on March 17.
  • The FF Open AI Day will showcase Faraday Future's current and future AI strategy and invite AI industry partners to jointly explore topics such as open-source ecosystems and AI applications in vertical sectors like mobility.
  • The company aims to advance end-to-end large-model autonomous driving development within an open-source framework, accelerate the development and launch of FF AI OS, and advance the integration of AI into its mechanical platforms.
  • FFIE +1.26% after hours to $1.6.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Capital Southwest appoints Michael Sarner as CEO
data.description:
• Capital Southwest Corporation (NASDAQ:CSWC) said on Tuesday that Chief Financial Officer Michael Sarner has been appointed by the Board of Directors to succeed Bowen Diehl as President and Chief Executive Officer of Capital Southwest.
  • Sarner has also been appointed to serve on the Board. Both appointments are effective February 17, 2025.
  • In addition, Chris Rehberger has been promoted from Executive Vice President of Finance and Treasurer to Chief Financial Officer, Treasurer & Secretary of the company, and Tabitha Geiger has been promoted from Deputy Compliance Officer to Chief Compliance Officer of the company, effective February 17, 2025.
  • Sarner joined Capital Southwest in 2015 and brings more than thirty years of financial, treasury and BDC experience to his new role.
